analysis.idx12_means: AlfaOBD internal ECU class number / cipher family ID. Matches dispatch table family_X keys.
analysis.idx14_means: Device type ID (more granular than family). Possibly maps to physical ECU role/CAN protocol variant.
analysis.cipher_dispatch_coverage: 8 of ~40 ECU families have cipher dispatch entries in algorithm-catalog.json. The other 32+ families use unmapped wrappers (W6 or W7 entries directly, or special non-W6/W7 ciphers).

bcm.cipher_dispatch: NOT IN DISPATCH TABLE — uses non-standard cipher
bcm.note: family_3 is NOT in the dispatch table — BCM uses a cipher not catalogued in the 8-family list. Possibly the ht linear algo with hardcoded constants, or a per-MY variant.

ecm.note: family_11 has 103 ECU variants but no dispatch entry. ECM uses per-variant cipher selection (could be one of the 380 W6 wrappers, identified at runtime).

rfhub.cipher_family_idx12: UNKNOWN — RFH not in catalog directly

rfh_diag_routines.1304: This procedure reads the PIN from the RFH memory. If the procedure terminates with an error retry with the ignition set to OFF or Lock position.
rfh_diag_routines.1306: This function will verify the functionality of the antenna coil located inside the RF-HUB. Ignition switch should be in the Run position while running the routine.
rfh_diag_routines.1367: RAM trucks only with high-line system. Use this function to program FOBIK(s) when the RF-HUB has been replaced or additional FOBIK(s) need to be added. The FOBIK(s) being pRograMmEd MUST bE
rfh_diag_routines.1520: RAM only. Use this function when the BCM has been replaced. It will create the BCM backup of the secret key value stored in the RF-HUB.
rfh_diag_routines.2508: governor output duty cycle for LSU heater 0

rfhub.note: RFH routines are runtime-dispatched (Method[2143] ir uses 2504-2508 as dict keys). The BCM↔RFH transfer is implemented as a BCM-family operation (idx[12]=3). For DIRECT AlfaOBD→RFH connections, the cipher likely uses family 18 (HSM/MSM/seats area) or a special non-W6/W7 path.
